textPlain: They might have to follow up with a “why we are never using <hosting> again”
As a Wise user, only for personal international transactions, I'm very curious to read this! I've had good experiences so far.

?
"Wise, formerly known as TransferWise, is an English financial technology company focused on global money transfers."
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wise_%28company%29
This was the best thing about Google cache. It's sad they killed it. I really wanted to read this.
If anyone opened this and still has it open, please paste the contents here so we can read it.

Has anyone ever just called them 'Wise'? Every single mention is 'Wise (formerly TransferWise)' like it's part of their legal entity name. Their CEO probably introduces himself as 'CEO of Wise (formerly TransferWise)'.
Are we being trolled. Is this deeply cynical humor. If so I love it
HN Hug of death knocked out the page. I copied all the text from an open tab and pasted it into google docs.https://docs.google.com/document/d/1YS8FLnSz2eP-nXp7FJR7Gsef...
HN hug of death just FYI
A 9 point HN post shouldn't bring down the server.

I do dozens of transactions every month sending payments to various freelancers. Been doing this for five years and can count the numbers of times I hand problems making payments on one hand - all we're minor and resolved in just a few days.

Also very curious, as a former Wise user who stopped using them after they demanded impossible KYC requirements (proof of address with address components that simply don't exist for my address, luckily with no money in the account, but didn't budge even when I reached out to support) and then seem to have silently dropped this months later (still not using them - not going to risk that they come back with more bullshit when they have money to hold hostage).Edit: Someone posted a copy of the article below, and it seems to be a similar issue with no satisfactory resolution.
Same here! I need to know!

The article details the story of a business that tried to change their address in NZ. Thier documents (while correct to NZ) didn’t match the criteria of Wise and then Wise effectively closed the account. The support and appeal process was basically nonexistent and even by the end article it is unclear that the business owner were even able to retrieve the funds in the Wise accounts. Also the author’s personal account with Wise was also closed.I hope this is a very brief overview of the article, which I would encourage people to read. In speaks to the huge imbalance in power and accountability in dealing with some companies

Bah. I posted it but I didn't keep it open on my phone. The tldr:They moved offices, informed wise of a change of address. Wise asked for proof of address. They sent a phone bill. Wise rejected it because the terminology of bills in New Zealand is different to the US (i.e. here bills are usually labelled Tax invoice rather than Bill). Wise support agent also made a barmey suggestion on how to get around it that they didn't follow. Another Wise agent called them back agreeing that the document should be accepted and resubmitted on their behalf. Later the document was rejected again and then their account was closed without any further communication from Wise. Later the author's personal wise account was closed just for being associated with the company.
I've only known it as just Wise
Not on archive.today or wayback either. (nor indexed by google, afaict)
